title: PC Buying Guide Guest Column: 'Take Her Up to 2.4 GHz, Mr. Scott'
author: Peter S. Kastner, InternetWeek (reblogged by Ray Schroeder, Techno-News Blog, University of Illinois at Springfield)
abstract: Ray Schroeder's Techno-News Blog (Jan 7 2003) reblog of Peter Kastner's InternetWeek PC buying-guide guest column titled 'Take Her Up to 2.4 GHz, Mr. Scott.' Kastner, writing in his InternetWeek column voice, argues that the 2.0-GHz Pentium 4 has been the corporate-mainstream standard for the past three months but is now being displaced: Dell, HP, and IBM have added 2.4-GHz Pentium 4 to their single-price lower-Pentium-4 price tier, meaning corporate IT managers can order a desktop with a 1.8-, 2.0-, 2.2-, or 2.4-GHz Pentium 4 for the exact same price at checkout. Classic Kastner practitioner-focused PC-procurement advice: take the faster chip, it's free. The Techno-News Blog is the preservation channel — Ray Schroeder (University of Illinois at Springfield OTEL editor) reblogged the Kastner column with attribution and source reference.
importance_rationale: Kastner-authored column (not an analyst quote in a third-party piece), preserved via the University of Illinois Techno-News Blog reblog. Represents Kastner's InternetWeek voice and his specific corporate-PC procurement advisory style during the Pentium 4 era. Important as an authored primary source rather than an analyst-quote-in-trade-press.
relevance_rationale: Specific 2003 Pentium 4 clock-speed advice is dated (Pentium 4 end-of-life 2008; clock-speed-as-spec mostly irrelevant since multi-core transition 2006). Kastner's underlying free-upgrade-at-the-same-price pattern recurs whenever vendors compete on single-price-tier upgrades (e.g., Apple M1 vs M2 MacBook Air at the same MSRP 2022).
prescience_rationale: Short buying-guide advisory, not a prediction; not-applicable for prescience rating.
